Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(&miroir_id) {\n534\t                    task.status = status;\n535\t                }\n536\t                Ok(())\n537\t            })\n538\t        })\n539\t    }\n540\t\n541\t    fn update_node_task(\n542\t        &self,\n543\t        miroir_id: &str,\n544\t        node_id: &str,\n545\t        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n546\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n547\t        let registry = self.clone();\n548\t        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n549\t        let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n550\t        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n551\t            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n552\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n553\t            rt.block_on(async move {\n554\t                let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n555\t                if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(&miroir_id) {\n556\t                    if let Some(node_task) = task.node_tasks.get_mut(&node_id) {\n557\t                        node_task.status = node_status;\n558\t                    }\n559\t                }\n560\t                Ok(())\n561\t            })\n562\t        })\n563\t    }\n564\t\n565\t    fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n566\t        let registry = self.clone();\n567\t        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n568\t            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n569\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n570\t            rt.block_on(async move {\n571\t                registry.list_async(&filter).await\n572\t            })\n573\t        })\n574\t    }\n575\t\n576\t    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n577\t        let registry = self.clone();\n578\t        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n579\t            let rt = match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\n580\t                Ok(rt) => rt,\n581\t                Err(_) => return 0,\n582\t            };\n583\t            rt.block_on(async move {\n584\t                registry.count().await\n585\t            })\n586\t        })\n587\t    }\n588\t}\n589\t\n590\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n591\t// TaskRegistryImpl — runtime-selected backend (Phase 3)\n592\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n593\t\n594\t/// Task status as lowercase string for store serialization.\n595\tfn status_to_str(s: TaskStatus) -> &'static str {\n596\t    match s {\n597\t        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n598\t        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n599\t        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n600\t        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n601\t        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n602\t    }\n603\t}\n604\t\n605\t/// Parse task status from store string.\n606\tfn str_to_status(s: &str) -> TaskStatus {\n607\t    match s {\n608\t        \"succeeded\" => TaskStatus::Succeeded,\n609\t        \"failed\" => TaskStatus::Failed,\n610\t        \"processing\" => TaskStatus::Processing,\n611\t        \"canceled\" => TaskStatus::Canceled,\n612\t        _ => TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n613\t    }\n614\t}\n615\t\n616\t/// Convert a store `TaskRow` to a `MiroirTask`.\n617\t///\n618\t/// Per-node status is not persisted; all nodes are set to `Enqueued`.\n619\t/// The GET /tasks/{id} endpoint polls nodes to reconstruct per-node status.\n620\tfn row_to_task(row: crate::task_store::TaskRow) -> MiroirTask {\n621\t    let node_tasks: HashMap<String, NodeTask> = row\n622\t        .node_tasks\n623\t        .into_iter()\n624\t        .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n625\t            (\n626\t                node_id,\n627\t                NodeTask {\n628\t                    task_uid,\n629\t                    status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n630\t                },\n631\t            )\n632\t        })\n633\t        .collect();\n634\t\n635\t    MiroirTask {\n636\t        miroir_id: row.miroir_id,\n637\t        created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n638\t        started_at: row.started_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n639\t        finished_at: row.finished_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n640\t        status: str_to_status(&row.status),\n641\t        index_uid: row.index_uid,\n642\t        task_type: row.task_type,\n643\t        node_tasks,\n644\t        error: row.error,\n645\t        node_errors: row.node_errors,\n646\t    }\n647\t}\n648\t\n649\t/// Convert a route `TaskFilter` to a store `TaskFilter`.\n650\tfn filter_to_store(f: &TaskFilter) -> crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n651\t    crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n652\t        status: f.status.map(|s| status_to_str(s).to_string()),\n653\t        index_uid: f.index_uid.clone(),\n654\t        task_type: f.task_type.clone(),\n655\t        limit: f.limit,\n656\t        offset: f.offset,\n657\t    }\n658\t}\n659\t\n660\t/// Runtime-selected task registry backend.\n661\t///\n662\t/// - `InMemory`: Phase 2 behaviour, tasks lost on restart (default for sqlite without path).\n663\t/// - `Sqlite`: persisted via `SqliteTaskStore`, survives restarts.\n664\t/// - `Redis`: persisted via `RedisTaskStore`, required for multi-replica HPA.\n665\t#[derive(Clone)]\n666\tpub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n667\t    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n668\t    Sqlite(Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>),\n669\t    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n670\t    Redis(Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>),\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\timpl TaskRegistryImpl {\n674\t    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n675\t    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n676\t        let store = crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore::open(std::path::Path::new(path))?;\n677\t        store.migrate()?;\n678\t        Ok(Self::Sqlite(Arc::new(store)))\n679\t    }\n680\t\n681\t    /// Open a Redis-backed registry at the given URL.\n682\t    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n683\t    pub fn redis(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n684\t        let store = tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n685\t            tokio::runtime::Handle::current()\n686\t                .block_on(crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore::open(url))\n687\t        })?;\n688\t        store.migrate()?;\n689\t        Ok(Self::Redis(Arc::new(store)))\n690\t    }\n691\t\n692\t    /// In-memory fallback (no persistence).\n693\t    pub fn in_memory() -> Self {\n694\t        Self::InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new())\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Return a reference to the underlying `SqliteTaskStore`, if this is the Sqlite variant.\n698\t    pub fn as_sqlite(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>> {\n699\t        match self {\n700\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(s) => Some(s),\n701\t            _ => None,\n702\t        }\n703\t    }\n704\t\n705\t    /// Return a reference to the underlying `RedisTaskStore`, if this is the Redis variant.\n706\t    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n707\t    pub fn as_redis(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>> {\n708\t        match self {\n709\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(r) => Some(r),\n710\t            _ => None,\n711\t        }\n712\t    }\n713\t\n714\t    /// Register a new task with metadata (sync, compatible with `TaskRegistry` trait).\n715\t    pub fn register_with_metadata(\n716\t        &self,\n717\t        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n718\t        index_uid: Option<String>,\n719\t        task_type: Option<String>,\n720\t    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n721\t        match self {\n722\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n723\t                crate::task::TaskRegistry::register_with_metadata(\n724\t                    r, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type,\n725\t                )\n726\t            }\n727\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n728\t                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n729\t                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n730\t                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n731\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n732\t                    .as_millis() as i64;\n733\t\n734\t                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n735\t                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n736\t                    created_at,\n737\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n738\t                    node_tasks,\n739\t                    error: None,\n740\t                    started_at: None,\n741\t                    finished_at: None,\n742\t                    index_uid,\n743\t                    task_type,\n744\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n745\t                };\n746\t\n747\t                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n748\t\n749\t                Ok(MiroirTask {\n750\t                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n751\t                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n752\t                    started_at: None,\n753\t                    finished_at: None,\n754\t                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n755\t                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n756\t                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n757\t                    node_tasks: new_task\n758\t                        .node_tasks\n759\t                        .into_iter()\n760\t                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n761\t                        .collect(),\n762\t                    error: None,\n763\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n764\t                })\n765\t            }\n766\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n767\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n768\t                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n769\t                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n770\t                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n771\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n772\t                    .as_millis() as i64;\n773\t\n774\t                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n775\t                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n776\t                    created_at,\n777\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n778\t                    node_tasks,\n779\t                    error: None,\n780\t                    started_at: None,\n781\t                    finished_at: None,\n782\t                    index_uid,\n783\t                    task_type,\n784\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n785\t                };\n786\t\n787\t                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n788\t\n789\t                Ok(MiroirTask {\n790\t                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n791\t                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n792\t                    started_at: None,\n793\t                    finished_at: None,\n794\t                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n795\t                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n796\t                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n797\t                    node_tasks: new_task\n798\t                        .node_tasks\n799\t                        .into_iter()\n800\t                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n801\t                        .collect(),\n802\t                    error: None,\n803\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n804\t                })\n805\t            }\n806\t        }\n807\t    }\n808\t\n809\t    /// Get a task by Miroir ID.\n810\t    pub fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n811\t        match self {\n812\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::get(r, miroir_id),\n813\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n814\t                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n815\t            }\n816\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n817\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n818\t                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n819\t            }\n820\t        }\n821\t    }\n822\t\n823\t    /// Update the overall task status.\n824\t    pub fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n825\t        match self {\n826\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n827\t                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_status(r, miroir_id, status)\n828\t            }\n829\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n830\t                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n831\t                Ok(())\n832\t            }\n833\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n834\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n835\t                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n836\t                Ok(())\n837\t            }\n838\t        }\n839\t    }\n840\t\n841\t    /// Update a node task's status.\n842\t    ///\n843\t    /// For persistent backends this is a no-op for per-node status since it is\n844\t    /// reconstructed from polling on each GET /tasks/{id}. The store only persists\n845\t    /// the overall task status and node task UIDs.\n846\t    pub fn update_node_task(\n847\t        &self,\n848\t        miroir_id: &str,\n849\t        node_id: &str,\n850\t        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n851\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n852\t        match self {\n853\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n854\t                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_node_task(r, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n855\t            }\n856\t            // Per-node status is reconstructed from polling on each GET; not persisted.\n857\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n858\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(_) => Ok(()),\n859\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(_) => Ok(()),\n860\t        }\n861\t    }\n862\t\n863\t    /// List tasks with optional filtering.\n864\t    pub fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n865\t        match self {\n866\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::list(r, filter),\n867\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n868\t                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n869\t                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n870\t                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n871\t            }\n872\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n873\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n874\t                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n875\t                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n876\t                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n877\t            }\n878\t        }\n879\t    }\n880\t\n881\t    /// Count total tasks.\n882\t    pub fn count(&self) -> usize {\n883\t        match self {\n884\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::count(r),\n885\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n886\t                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n887\t            }\n888\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n889\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n890\t                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n891\t            }\n892\t        }\n893\t    }\n894\t\n895\t    /// Set error on a task.\n896\t    pub fn set_error(&self, miroir_id: &str, error: &str) -> Result<()> {\n897\t        match self {\n898\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(()),\n899\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n900\t                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n901\t                Ok(())\n902\t            }\n903\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n904\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n905\t                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n906\t                Ok(())\n907\t            }\n908\t        }\n909\t    }\n910\t}\n911\t\n912\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n913\timpl crate::task::TaskRegistry for TaskRegistryImpl {\n914\t    fn register_with_metadata(\n915\t        &self,\n916\t        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n917\t        index_uid: Option<String>,\n918\t        task_type: Option<String>,\n919\t    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n920\t        TaskRegistryImpl::register_with_metadata(self, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type)\n921\t    }\n922\t\n923\t    fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n924\t        TaskRegistryImpl::get(self, miroir_id)\n925\t    }\n926\t\n927\t    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n928\t        TaskRegistryImpl::update_status(self, miroir_id, status)\n929\t    }\n930\t\n931\t    fn TaskRegistryImpl::update_node_task(self, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n938\t    }\n939\t\n940\t    fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n941\t {\n951\t    use super::*;\n952\t    #[test]\n953\t    fn test_in_memory_register_creates_task() {\n954\t        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n955\t        let registry =  #[test]\n1025\t    fn test_in_memory_update_node_task() {\n1026\t        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n1027\t        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n1028\t = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n1055\t        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n1056\t        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n1057\t Task registry: manages Miroir task namespace.\n//!\n//! Phase 3: persistent backends (SQLite, Redis) alongside in-memory fallback.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse crate::task::{MiroirTask, NodeTask, NodeTaskStatus, TaskStatus, TaskFilter};\nuse crate::error::MiroirError;\nuse crate::scatter::NodeClient;\nuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\nuse crate::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// In-memory task registry implementation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    tasks: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, MiroirTask>>>,\n}\n\n/// Trait for node polling operations.\n/// Allows the task registry to poll nodes without tight coupling to HTTP client.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\npub trait NodePoller: Send + Sync {\n    /// Poll a single node for task status.\n    async fn poll_node_task(\n        &self,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        task_uid: u64,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<NodeTaskStatus, String>;\n}\n\n/// Node poller implementation using a NodeClient and Topology.\npub struct ClientNodePoller<C: NodeClient> {\n    client: Arc<C>,\n    topology: Arc<Topology>,\n}\n\nimpl<C: NodeClient> ClientNodePoller<C> {\n    /// Create a new node poller with the given client and topology.\n    pub fn new(client: Arc<C>, topology: Arc<Topology>) -> Self {\n        Self { client, topology }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl<C: NodeClient> NodePoller for ClientNodePoller<C> {\n    async fn poll_node_task(\n        &self,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        task_uid: u64,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<NodeTaskStatus, String> {\n        use crate::scatter::TaskStatusRequest;\n\n        let req = TaskStatusRequest { task_uid };\n        self.client\n            .get_task_status(node_id, address, &req)\n            .await\n            .map(|resp| resp.to_node_status())\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"{:?}\", e))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    /// Create a new in-memory task registry.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            tasks: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with the given node tasks.\n    pub async fn register_async(&self, node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        self.register_async_with_metadata(node_tasks, None, None).await\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with the given node tasks and metadata.\n    pub async fn register_async_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n        let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n            .as_millis() as u64;\n\n        let mut tasks = HashMap::new();\n        for (node_id, task_uid) in node_tasks {\n            tasks.insert(node_id, NodeTask {\n                task_uid,\n                status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            });\n        }\n\n        let task = MiroirTask {\n            miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n            created_at,\n            started_at: None,\n            finished_at: None,\n            status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            index_uid,\n            task_type,\n            node_tasks: tasks,\n            error: None,\n            node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n        };\n\n        // Insert the task\n        {\n            let mut registry = self.tasks.write().await;\n            registry.insert(miroir_id.clone(), task.clone());\n        }\n\n        // Spawn a background task to poll for status updates (simulated for Phase 2)\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id_clone = miroir_id.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            registry.poll_task_status_simulated(&miroir_id_clone).await;\n        });\n\n        Ok(task)\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with the given node tasks and metadata, with real node polling.\n    ///\n    /// This version takes a NodePoller implementation to actually poll nodes for status updates.\n    pub async fn register_with_poller<P: NodePoller + 'static>(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n        poller: Arc<P>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n        let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n            .as_millis() as u64;\n\n        let mut tasks = HashMap::new();\n        for (node_id, task_uid) in node_tasks {\n            tasks.insert(node_id.clone(), NodeTask {\n                task_uid,\n                status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            });\n        }\n\n        let task = MiroirTask {\n            miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n            created_at,\n            started_at: None,\n            finished_at: None,\n            status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            index_uid,\n            task_type,\n            node_tasks: tasks.clone(),\n            error: None,\n            node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n        };\n\n        // Insert the task\n        {\n            let mut registry = self.tasks.write().await;\n            registry.insert(miroir_id.clone(), task.clone());\n        }\n\n        // Spawn a background task to poll for status updates using real node polling\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id_clone = miroir_id.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            registry.poll_task_status_with_poller(&miroir_id_clone, poller).await;\n        });\n\n        Ok(task)\n    }\n\n    /// Get task by ID (async version).\n    pub async fn get_async(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Option<MiroirTask> {\n        let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n        tasks.get(miroir_id).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Delete a task from the registry.\n    pub async fn delete(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        Ok(tasks.remove(miroir_id).is_some())\n    }\n\n    /// Count total tasks in the registry.\n    pub async fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n        tasks.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Prune old tasks (in-memory only, for Phase 3 this will use durable storage).\n    pub async fn prune_old_tasks(&self, _cutoff_ms: u64) -> Result<usize> {\n        // In-memory implementation: no pruning in Phase 2\n        // Phase 3 will add durable storage and pruning\n        Ok(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Update the overall task status based on node task statuses.\n    pub async fn update_overall_status(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        let task = match tasks.get(miroir_id) {\n            Some(t) => t.clone(),\n            None => return Ok(false),\n        };\n\n        // Determine overall status from node tasks\n        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n        let mut any_failed = false;\n        let mut all_terminal = true;\n\n        for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n            match node_task.status {\n                NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                    all_terminal = false;\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                }\n                NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n                    any_failed = true;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let new_status = if any_failed {\n            TaskStatus::Failed\n        } else if all_terminal && all_succeeded {\n            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n        } else if !all_terminal {\n            TaskStatus::Processing\n        } else {\n            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n        };\n\n        if new_status != task.status {\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                t.status = new_status;\n            }\n            Ok(true)\n        } else {\n            Ok(false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status.\n    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n    ///\n    /// Phase 2: Simulates node polling (tasks complete after ~500ms)\n    /// Phase 3: Will poll actual nodes via HttpClient using topology\n    async fn poll_task_status_simulated(&self, miroir_id: &str) {\n        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n\n        loop {\n            // Get the current task state\n            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n\n            let task = match task {\n                Some(t) => t,\n                None => return, // Task was deleted\n            };\n\n            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // In a real implementation, we would query the nodes here\n            // For Phase 2, we simulate status progression\n            // Phase 3 will add actual node polling via HttpClient\n\n            // Check each node task's status\n            let mut all_terminal = true;\n            for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n                match node_task.status {\n                    NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                        all_terminal = false;\n                    }\n                    NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {}\n                }\n            }\n\n            // For testing purposes, simulate tasks completing\n            // In production, this would poll actual nodes\n            if !all_terminal && delay_ms >= 500 {\n                // Simulate completion for testing\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &mut t.node_tasks {\n                        if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing) {\n                            node_task.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Update overall status\n                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n                    let mut any_failed = false;\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n                        match node_task.status {\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                                all_succeeded = false;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if any_failed {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n                    } else if all_succeeded {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                    } else {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                    }\n                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                            .unwrap_or_default()\n                            .as_millis() as u64);\n                    }\n                }\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status, using real node polling.\n    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n    async fn poll_task_status_with_poller<P: NodePoller>(&self, miroir_id: &str, poller: Arc<P>) {\n        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n\n        loop {\n            // Get the current task state\n            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n\n            let task = match task {\n                Some(t) => t,\n                None => return, // Task was deleted\n            };\n\n            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Collect node IDs and task UIDs to poll\n            let node_polls: Vec<(NodeId, u64)> = task.node_tasks\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|(_, nt)| !matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed))\n                .map(|(node_id, nt)| (NodeId::new(node_id.clone()), nt.task_uid))\n                .collect();\n\n            if node_polls.is_empty() {\n                // All node tasks are terminal, update overall status\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n                    let mut any_failed = false;\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n                        match node_task.status {\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                                all_succeeded = false;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if any_failed {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n                    } else if all_succeeded {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                    } else {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                    }\n                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                            .unwrap_or_default()\n                            .as_millis() as u64);\n                    }\n                }\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Poll each node for status\n            let mut node_statuses = HashMap::new();\n            for (node_id, task_uid) in &node_polls {\n                // Get node address from topology (would need topology here)\n                // For now, use a mock address - in production, this would come from the topology\n                let address = format!(\"http://{}\", node_id.as_str());\n\n                match poller.poll_node_task(&node_id, &address, *task_uid).await {\n                    Ok(status) => {\n                        node_statuses.insert(node_id.clone(), status);\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\"Failed to poll node {} for task {}: {}\", node_id, task_uid, e);\n                        // On poll failure, keep the current status but mark for potential degradation\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Update node task statuses\n            {\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    for (node_id, status) in node_statuses {\n                        if let Some(node_task) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id.as_str()) {\n                            node_task.status = status;\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    // Update started_at timestamp if moving to processing\n                    if t.status == TaskStatus::Enqueued {\n                        let any_processing = t.node_tasks.values().any(|nt| {\n                            matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Processing)\n                        });\n                        if any_processing && t.started_at.is_none() {\n                            t.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                                .unwrap_or_default()\n                                .as_millis() as u64);\n                            t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List tasks with optional filtering (Meilisearch-compatible).\n    pub async fn list_async(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        let guard = self.tasks.read().await;\n        let mut result: Vec<MiroirTask> = guard.values().cloned().collect();\n\n        // Apply status filter\n        if let Some(status) = filter.status {\n            result.retain(|t| t.status == status);\n        }\n\n        // Apply index_uid filter\n        if let Some(index_uid) = &filter.index_uid {\n            result.retain(|t| t.index_uid.as_ref().map_or(false, |uid| uid == index_uid));\n        }\n\n        // Apply task_type filter\n        if let Some(task_type) = &filter.task_type {\n            result.retain(|t| t.task_type.as_ref().map_or(false, |ty| ty == task_type));\n        }\n\n        // Apply offset\n        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n            if offset < result.len() {\n                result = result[offset..].to_vec();\n            } else {\n                result.clear();\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Apply limit\n        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n            result.truncate(limit);\n        }\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Stub TaskRegistry implementation for compatibility.\n/// This delegates to the async methods via tokio::task::block_in_place.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl crate::task::TaskRegistry for InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.register_async_with_metadata(node_tasks, index_uid, task_type).await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                Ok(registry.get_async(&miroir_id).await)\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(&miroir_id) {\n                    task.status = status;\n                }\n                Ok(())\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n        let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(&miroir_id) {\n                    if let Some(node_task) = task.node_tasks.get_mut(&node_id) {\n                        node_task.status = node_status;\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(())\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.list_async(&filter).await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\n                Ok(rt) => rt,\n                Err(_) => return 0,\n            };\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.count().await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// TaskRegistryImpl — runtime-selected backend (Phase 3)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Task status as lowercase string for store serialization.\nfn status_to_str(s: TaskStatus) -> &'static str {\n    match s {\n        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parse task status from store string.\nfn str_to_status(s: &str) -> TaskStatus {\n    match s {\n        \"succeeded\" => TaskStatus::Succeeded,\n        \"failed\" => TaskStatus::Failed,\n        \"processing\" => TaskStatus::Processing,\n        \"canceled\" => TaskStatus::Canceled,\n        _ => TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert a store `TaskRow` to a `MiroirTask`.\n///\n/// Per-node status is not persisted; all nodes are set to `Enqueued`.\n/// The GET /tasks/{id} endpoint polls nodes to reconstruct per-node status.\nfn row_to_task(row: crate::task_store::TaskRow) -> MiroirTask {\n    let node_tasks: HashMap<String, NodeTask> = row\n        .node_tasks\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n            (\n                node_id,\n                NodeTask {\n                    task_uid,\n                    status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                },\n            )\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirTask {\n        miroir_id: row.miroir_id,\n        created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n        started_at: row.started_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n        finished_at: row.finished_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n        status: str_to_status(&row.status),\n        index_uid: row.index_uid,\n        task_type: row.task_type,\n        node_tasks,\n        error: row.error,\n        node_errors: row.node_errors,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert a route `TaskFilter` to a store `TaskFilter`.\nfn filter_to_store(f: &TaskFilter) -> crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n    crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n        status: f.status.map(|s| status_to_str(s).to_string()),\n        index_uid: f.index_uid.clone(),\n        task_type: f.task_type.clone(),\n        limit: f.limit,\n        offset: f.offset,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Runtime-selected task registry backend.\n///\n/// - `InMemory`: Phase 2 behaviour, tasks lost on restart (default for sqlite without path).\n/// - `Sqlite`: persisted via `SqliteTaskStore`, survives restarts.\n/// - `Redis`: persisted via `RedisTaskStore`, required for multi-replica HPA.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n    Sqlite(Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>),\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    Redis(Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>),\n}\n\nimpl TaskRegistryImpl {\n    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore::open(std::path::Path::new(path))?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Sqlite(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// Open a Redis-backed registry at the given URL.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn redis(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            tokio::runtime::Handle::current()\n                .block_on(crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore::open(url))\n        })?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Redis(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// In-memory fallback (no persistence).\n    pub fn in_memory() -> Self {\n        Self::InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new())\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `SqliteTaskStore`, if this is the Sqlite variant.\n    pub fn as_sqlite(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(s) => Some(s),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `RedisTaskStore`, if this is the Redis variant.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn as_redis(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(r) => Some(r),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with metadata (sync, compatible with `TaskRegistry` trait).\n    pub fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::register_with_metadata(\n                    r, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type,\n                )\n            }\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as i64;\n\n                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n                    created_at,\n                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n                    node_tasks,\n                    error: None,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    index_uid,\n                    task_type,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                };\n\n                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n\n                Ok(MiroirTask {\n                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n                    node_tasks: new_task\n                        .node_tasks\n                        .into_iter()\n                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n                        .collect(),\n                    error: None,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                })\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as i64;\n\n                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n                    created_at,\n                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n                    node_tasks,\n                    error: None,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    index_uid,\n                    task_type,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                };\n\n                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n\n                Ok(MiroirTask {\n                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n                    node_tasks: new_task\n                        .node_tasks\n                        .into_iter()\n                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n                        .collect(),\n                    error: None,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                })\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a task by Miroir ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::get(r, miroir_id),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update the overall task status.\n    pub fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_status(r, miroir_id, status)\n            }\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update a node task's status.\n    ///\n    /// For persistent backends this is a no-op for per-node status since it is\n    /// reconstructed from polling on each GET /tasks/{id}. The store only persists\n    /// the overall task status and node task UIDs.\n    pub fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_node_task(r, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n            }\n            // Per-node status is reconstructed from polling on each GET; not persisted.\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(_) => Ok(()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List tasks with optional filtering.\n    pub fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::list(r, filter),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Count total tasks.\n    pub fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::count(r),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set error on a task.\n    pub fn set_error(&self, miroir_id: &str, error: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl crate::task::TaskRegistry for TaskRegistryImpl {\n    fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::register_with_metadata(self, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type)\n    }\n\n    fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::get(self, miroir_id)\n    }\n\n    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::update_status(self, miroir_id, status)\n    }\n\n    fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::update_node_task(self, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n    }\n\n    fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::list(self, filter)\n    }\n\n    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::count(self)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_register_creates_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert!(task.miroir_id.starts_with(\"mtask-\"));\n        assert_eq!(task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued);\n        assert_eq!(task.node_tasks.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_get_returns_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.unwrap().miroir_id, task.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_list_filters_by_status() {\n        let rt =        let (task1, _task2) = rt.block_on(async {\n            let t1 = registry.register_async(node_tasks.clone()).await.unwrap();\n            let t2 = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n            (t1, t2)\n        });\n\n        // Update task1 to succeeded - must be done within runtime context\n        let task1_id = task1.miroir_id.clone();\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(&task1_id) {\n                t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n            }\n        });\n\n        let filter = TaskFilter {\n            status: Some(TaskStatus::Succeeded),\n            node_id: None,\n            index_uid: None,\n }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(tasks.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(tasks[0].miroir_id, task1.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_update_node_task() {\n        let rt =    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_update_overall_status() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        // Mark one node as succeeded, one as processing - must be done within runtime context\n        let task_id = task.miroir_id.clone();\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(&task_id) {\n                if let Some(nt)       nt.status = NodeTaskStatus::Processing;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Overall status should still be enqueued/processing\n        let updated = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.update_overall_status(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert!(updated);\n\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.status, TaskStatus::Processing);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_list_filters_by_index_uid() = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 3);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        // Wait for task to complete (simulated exponential backoff: 25 + 50 + 100 + 200 + 400 = 775ms)\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(800)).await;\n        });\n\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded);\n        assert!(retrieved.finished_at.is_some());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_miroir_task_id_format() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n          task_type: Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n            limit: None,\n            offset: None,\n        };\n\n        let tasks = rt.block_on(async {\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b07b5c21ca3d4b599b2bc4c3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Schema migration system for TaskStore backends.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Migrations are numbered SQL files embedded in the binary at compile time.\n4\t//! Each migration is applied exactly once, in order, and tracked in the\n5\t//! schema_versions table (SQLite) or schema_version key (Redis).\n6\t//!\n7\t//! ## Version checking\n8\t//!\n9\t//! - Binary version = max migration number compiled into the binary\n10\t//! - Store version = max migration applied to the store\n11\t//! - On startup: if store version > binary version, refuse to start with\n12\t//!   MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead — operator must restore from backup or\n13\t//!   upgrade the binary.\n14\t//! - If binary version > store version, apply pending migrations.\n15\t\n16\tuse crate::Result;\n17\tuse std::collections::BTreeMap;\n18\t\n19\t/// A single migration with its version number and SQL content.\n20\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n21\tpub struct Migration {\n22\t    /// Monotonic version number (e.g., 1 for 001_initial.sql)\n23\t    pub version: i64,\n24\t    /// Raw SQL to execute for this migration\n25\t    pub sql: &'static str,\n26\t}\n27\t\n28\t/// Registry of all available migrations compiled into the binary.\n29\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n30\tpub struct MigrationRegistry {\n31\t    /// Ordered map of version -> migration\n32\t    migrations: BTreeMap<i64, Migration>,\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\timpl MigrationRegistry {\n36\t    /// Create a new registry from a list of migrations.\n37\t    ///\n38\t    /// Panics if migration versions are not unique.\n39\t    pub fn new(migrations: &[Migration]) -> Self {\n40\t        let mut map = BTreeMap::new();\n41\t        for m in migrations {\n42\t            if map.insert(m.version, m.clone()).is_some() {\n43\t                panic!(\"duplicate migration version: {}\", m.version);\n44\t            }\n45\t        }\n46\t        Self { migrations: map }\n47\t    }\n48\t\n49\t    /// Get the maximum migration version (the binary's schema version).\n50\t    pub fn max_version(&self) -> i64 {\n51\t        self.migrations.keys().last().copied().unwrap_or(0)\n52\t    }\n53\t\n54\t    /// Get all migrations that need to be applied to reach the target version.\n55\t    ///\n56\t    /// Returns migrations with version > `current_version` in ascending order.\n57\t    pub fn pending_migrations(&self, current_version: i64) -> Vec<&Migration> {\n58\t        self.migrations\n59\t            .values()\n60\t            .filter(|m| m.version > current_version)\n61\t            .collect()\n62\t    }\n63\t\n64\t    /// Validate that the store version is not ahead of the binary version.\n65\t    ///\n66\t    /// Returns an error if store_version > binary_version.\n67\t    pub fn validate_version(&self, store_version: i64) -> Result<()> {\n68\t        let binary_version = self.max_version();\n69\t        if store_version > binary_version {\n70\t            return Err(crate::MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n71\t                store_version,\n72\t                binary_version,\n73\t            });\n74\t        }\n75\t        Ok(())\n76\t    }\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\t/// Build the migration registry from embedded migration files.\n80\t///\n81\t/// This function is generated by the macro that embeds migration files.\n82\t/// For now, we manually construct the registry with the available migrations.\n83\tpub fn build_registry() -> MigrationRegistry {\n84\t    MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n85\t        Migration {\n86\t            version: 1,\n87\t            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/001_initial.sql\"),\n88\t        },\n89\t        Migration {\n90\t            version: 2,\n91\t            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/002_feature_tables.sql\"),\n92\t        },\n93\t        Migration {\n94\t            version: 3,\n95\t            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/003_task_registry_fields.sql\"),\n96\t        },\n97\t    ])\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\t#[cfg(test)]\n101\tmod tests {\n102\t    use super::*;\n103\t\n104\t    #[test]\n105\t    fn test_registry_max_version() {\n106\t        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n107\t            Migration {\n108\t                version: 1,\n109\t                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n110\t            },\n111\t            Migration {\n112\t                version: 2,\n113\t                sql: \"ALTER TABLE foo ADD COLUMN name TEXT;\",\n114\t            },\n115\t        ]);\n116\t\n117\t        assert_eq!(registry.max_version(), 2);\n118\t    }\n119\t\n120\t    #[test]\n121\t    fn test_pending_migrations() {\n122\t        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n123\t            Migration {\n124\t                version: 1,\n125\t                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n126\t            },\n127\t            Migration {\n128\t                version: 2,\n129\t                sql: \"ALTER TABLE foo ADD COLUMN name TEXT;\",\n130\t            },\n131\t            Migration {\n132\t                version: 3,\n133\t                sql: \"CREATE INDEX idx_foo_name ON foo(name);\",\n134\t            },\n135\t        ]);\n136\t\n137\t        // No pending migrations when at max version\n138\t        assert!(registry.pending_migrations(3).is_empty());\n139\t\n140\t        // All migrations pending when at version 0\n141\t        let pending = registry.pending_migrations(0);\n142\t        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 3);\n143\t        assert_eq!(pending[0].version, 1);\n144\t        assert_eq!(pending[1].version, 2);\n145\t        assert_eq!(pending[2].version, 3);\n146\t\n147\t        // Schema migration system for TaskStore backends.\n//!\n//! Migrations are numbered SQL files embedded in the binary at compile time.\n//! Each migration is applied exactly once, in order, and tracked in the\n//! schema_versions table (SQLite) or schema_version key (Redis).\n//!\n//! ## Version checking\n//!\n//! - Binary version = max migration number compiled into the binary\n//! - Store version = max migration applied to the store\n//! - On startup: if store version > binary version, refuse to start with\n//!   MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead — operator must restore from backup or\n//!   upgrade the binary.\n//! - If binary version > store version, apply pending migrations.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse std::collections::BTreeMap;\n\n/// A single migration with its version number and SQL content.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct Migration {\n    /// Monotonic version number (e.g., 1 for 001_initial.sql)\n    pub version: i64,\n    /// Raw SQL to execute for this migration\n    pub sql: &'static str,\n}\n\n/// Registry of all available migrations compiled into the binary.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MigrationRegistry {\n    /// Ordered map of version -> migration\n    migrations: BTreeMap<i64, Migration>,\n}\n\nimpl MigrationRegistry {\n    /// Create a new registry from a list of migrations.\n    ///\n    /// Panics if migration versions are not unique.\n    pub fn new(migrations: &[Migration]) -> Self {\n        let mut map = BTreeMap::new();\n        for m in migrations {\n            if map.insert(m.version, m.clone()).is_some() {\n                panic!(\"duplicate migration version: {}\", m.version);\n            }\n        }\n        Self { migrations: map }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the maximum migration version (the binary's schema version).\n    pub fn max_version(&self) -> i64 {\n        self.migrations.keys().last().copied().unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all migrations that need to be applied to reach the target version.\n    ///\n    /// Returns migrations with version > `current_version` in ascending order.\n    pub fn pending_migrations(&self, current_version: i64) -> Vec<&Migration> {\n        self.migrations\n            .values()\n            .filter(|m| m.version > current_version)\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Validate that the store version is not ahead of the binary version.\n    ///\n    /// Returns an error if store_version > binary_version.\n    pub fn validate_version(&self, store_version: i64) -> Result<()> {\n        let binary_version = self.max_version();\n        if store_version > binary_version {\n            return Err(crate::MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n                store_version,\n                binary_version,\n            });\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build the migration registry from embedded migration files.\n///\n/// This function is generated by the macro that embeds migration files.\n/// For now, we manually construct the registry with the available migrations.\npub fn build_registry() -> MigrationRegistry {\n    MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n        Migration {\n            version: 1,\n            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/001_initial.sql\"),\n        },\n        Migration {\n            version: 2,\n            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/002_feature_tables.sql\"),\n        },\n        Migration {\n            version: 3,\n            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/003_task_registry_fields.sql\"),\n        },\n    ])\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_registry_max_version() {\n        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n            Migration {\n                version: 1,\n                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n            },\n            Migration {\n                version: 2,\n                sql: \"ALTER TABLE foo ADD COLUMN name TEXT;\",\n            },\n        ]);\n\n        assert_eq!(registry.max_version(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_pending_migrations() {\n        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n            Migration {\n                version: 1,\n                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n            },\n            Migration {\n                version: 2,\n                sql: \"ALTER TABLE foo ADD COLUMN name TEXT;\",\n            },\n            Migration {\n                version: 3,\n                sql: \"CREATE INDEX idx_foo_name ON foo(name);\",\n            },\n        ]);\n\n        // No pending migrations when at max version\n        assert!(registry.pending_migrations(3).is_empty());\n\n        // All migrations pending when at version 0\n        let pending = registry.pending_migrations(0);\n        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 3);\n        assert_eq!(pending[0].version, 1);\n        assert_eq!(pending[1].version, 2);\n        assert_eq!(pending[2].version, 3);\n\n        // Only migrations 2 and 3 pending when at version 1\n        let pending = registry.pending_migrations(1);\n        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(pending[0].version, 2);\n        assert_eq!(pending[1].version, 3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_version_success() {\n        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[Migration {\n            version: 5,\n            sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n        }]);\n\n        // Store at same version is OK\n        registry.validate_version(5).unwrap();\n\n        // Store behind binary is OK (will apply migrations)\n        registry.validate_version(3).unwrap();\n\n        // Fresh store (version 0) is OK\n        registry.validate_version(0).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_version_store_ahead() {\n        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[Migration {\n            version: 3,\n            sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n        }]);\n\n        // Store ahead of binary should fail\n        let result = registry.validate_version(5);\n        assert!(result.is_err());\n        match result.unwrap_err() {\n            crate::MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n                store_version,\n                binary_version,\n            } => {\n                assert_eq!(store_version, 5);\n                assert_eq!(binary_version, 3);\n            }\n            _ => panic!(\"expected SchemaVersionAhead error\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    #[should_panic(expected = \"duplicate migration version\")]\n    fn test_duplicate_version_panics() {\n        MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n            Migration {\n                version: 1,\n                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n            },\n            Migration {\n                version: 1,\n                sql: \"CREATE TABLE bar (id INT);\",\n            },\n        ]);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":208,"startLine":1,"totalLines":208}}}
