Tenant-to-replica group affinity (plan §13.15).\n//!\n//! Provides noisy-neighbor isolation for multi-tenant deployments by routing\n//! tenant queries to pinned replica groups.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::topology::Topology;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, warn};\n\n/// Tenant affinity configuration (plan §13.15).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TenantAffinityConfig {\n    /// Whether tenant affinity is enabled.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Resolution mode: \"header\", \"api_key\", or \"explicit\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_mode\")]\n    pub mode: String,\n    /// Header name for header mode.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_header_name\")]\n    pub header_name: String,\n    /// Fallback mode when tenant cannot be resolved: \"hash\", \"random\", or \"reject\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_fallback\")]\n    pub fallback: String,\n    /// Static tenant -> group mapping (explicit mode).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub static_map: HashMap<String, u32>,\n    /// Dedicated groups (no other tenants allowed).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub dedicated_groups: Vec<u32>,\n}\n\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_mode() -> String {\n    \"header\".to_string()\n}\nfn default_header_name() -> String {\n    \"X-Miroir-Tenant\".to_string()\n}\nfn default_fallback() -> String {\n    \"hash\".to_string()\n}\n\nimpl Default for TenantAffinityConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            mode: default_mode(),\n            header_name: default_header_name(),\n            fallback: default_fallback(),\n            static_map: HashMap::new(),\n            dedicated_groups: vec![],\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Resolution mode.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum ResolutionMode {\n    Header,\n    ApiKey,\n    Explicit,\n}\n\nimpl ResolutionMode {\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"header\" => Some(Self::Header),\n            \"api_key\" => Some(Self::ApiKey),\n            \"explicit\" => Some(Self::Explicit),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Fallback mode.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum FallbackMode {\n    Hash,\n    Random,\n    Reject,\n}\n\nimpl FallbackMode {\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"hash\" => Some(Self::Hash),\n            \"random\" => Some(Self::Random),\n            \"reject\" => Some(Self::Reject),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Tenant affinity manager.\npub struct TenantAffinityManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: TenantAffinityConfig,\n    /// API key -> tenant mapping (api_key mode).\n    api_key_map: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, String>>>,\n    /// Topology for computing group counts.\n    topology: Arc<Topology>,\n}\n\nimpl TenantAffinityManager {\n    /// Create a new tenant affinity manager.\n    pub fn new(config: TenantAffinityConfig, topology: Arc<Topology>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            api_key_map: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            topology,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Resolve a tenant ID from request metadata.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the tenant ID, or None if no tenant can be resolved.\n    pub fn resolve_tenant(\n        &self,\n        headers: &HashMap<String, String>,\n        api_key: Option<&str>,\n    ) -> Option<String> {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let mode = ResolutionMode::from_str(self.config.mode.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(ResolutionMode::Header);\n\n        match mode {\n            ResolutionMode::Header => {\n                headers.get(&self.config.header_name).cloned()\n            }\n            ResolutionMode::ApiKey => {\n                // Look up tenant from API key\n                api_key.and_then(|key| {\n                    // In real implementation, this would query the api_key_map\n                    None\n                })\n            }\n            ResolutionMode::Explicit => {\n                // In explicit mode, tenant must be in static_map\n                // This is typically used for predefined tenant-to-group mappings\n                None\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the target group for a tenant.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the group ID, or None if the tenant cannot be resolved.\n    pub fn get_group_for_tenant(&self, tenant_id: &str) -> Option<u32> {\n        // Check static map first\n        if let Some(&group) = self.config.static_map.get(tenant_id) {\n            return Some(group);\n        }\n\n        // Hash-based assignment: hash(tenant_id) % replica_group_count\n        let rg_count = self.topology.replica_group_count();\n        if rg_count == 0 {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        use std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;\n        use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n\n        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n        tenant_id.hash(&mut hasher);\n        let hash = hasher.finish();\n        Some((hash % rg_count as u64) as u32)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the fallback group for an unknown tenant.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if fallback mode is \"reject\".\n    pub fn get_fallback_group(&self, tenant_id: Option<&str>) -> Result<Option<u32>> {\n        let fallback = FallbackMode::from_str(self.config.fallback.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(FallbackMode::Hash);\n\n        match fallback {\n            FallbackMode::Hash => {\n                let rg_count = self.topology.replica_group_count();\n                if rg_count == 0 {\n                    return Ok(None);\n                }\n\n                let id_to_hash = tenant_id.unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n\n                use std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;\n                use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n\n                let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n                id_to_hash.hash(&mut hasher);\n                let hash = hasher.finish();\n                Ok(Some((hash % rg_count as u64) as u32))\n            }\n            FallbackMode::Random => {\n                let rg_count = self.topology.replica_group_count();\n                if rg_count == 0 {\n                    return Ok(None);\n                }\n                use rand::Rng;\n                let mut rng = rand::thread_rng();\n                Ok(Some(rng.gen_range(0..rg_count as u32) as u32))\n            }\n            FallbackMode::Reject => {\n                Err(MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\n                    \"tenant {} not found\",\n                    tenant_id.unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n                )))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Resolve the target group for a request.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the group ID to route to, or an error if tenant cannot be resolved\n    /// and fallback mode is \"reject\".\n    pub fn resolve_group(\n        &self,\n        headers: &HashMap<String, String>,\n        api_key: Option<&str>,\n    ) -> Result<Option<u32>> {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        let tenant_id = match self.resolve_tenant(headers, api_key) {\n            Some(id) => id,\n            None => return self.get_fallback_group(None),\n        };\n\n        match self.get_group_for_tenant(&tenant_id) {\n            Some(group) => Ok(Some(group)),\n            None => self.get_fallback_group(Some(&tenant_id)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group is dedicated (only mapped tenants allowed).\n    pub fn is_dedicated_group(&self, group_id: u32) -> bool {\n        self.config.dedicated_groups.contains(&group_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Set the API key -> tenant mapping.\n    pub async fn set_api_key_mapping(&self, api_key: String, tenant_id: String) {\n        let mut map = self.api_key_map.write().await;\n        map.insert(api_key, tenant_id);\n    }\n\n    /// Get tenant for an API key.\n    pub async fn get_tenant_for_api_key(&self, api_key: &str) -> Option<String> {\n        let map = self.api_key_map.read().await;\n        map.get(api_key).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a tenant is allowed to use a group.\n    pub fn is_tenant_allowed_in_group(&self, tenant_id: &str, group_id: u32) -> bool {\n        // If group is not dedicated, any tenant can use it\n        if !self.is_dedicated_group(group_id) {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        // If group is dedicated, check static map\n        match self.get_group_for_tenant(tenant_id) {\n            Some(mapped_group) => mapped_group == group_id,\n            None => false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for TenantAffinityManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(TenantAffinityConfig::default(), Arc::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)))\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = TenantAffinityConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.enabled);\n        assert_eq!(config.mode, \"header\");\n        assert_eq!(config.header_name, \"X-Miroir-Tenant\");\n        assert_eq!(config.fallback, \"hash\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_resolution_mode_from_str() {\n        assert_eq!(ResolutionMode::from_str(\"header\"), Some(ResolutionMode::Header));\n        assert_eq!(ResolutionMode::from_str(\"api_key\"), Some(ResolutionMode::ApiKey));\n        assert_eq!(ResolutionMode::from_str(\"explicit\"), Some(ResolutionMode::Explicit));\n        assert_eq!(ResolutionMode::from_str(\"unknown\"), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_fallback_mode_from_str() {\n        assert_eq!(FallbackMode::from_str(\"hash\"), Some(FallbackMode::Hash));\n        assert_eq!(FallbackMode::from_str(\"random\"), Some(FallbackMode::Random));\n        assert_eq!(FallbackMode::from_str(\"reject\"), Some(FallbackMode::Reject));\n        assert_eq!(FallbackMode::from_str(\"unknown\"), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_resolve_tenant_header() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 3, 1));\n        let config = TenantAffinityConfig {\n            mode: \"header\".to_string(),\n            header_name: \"X-Tenant\".to_string(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(config, topology);\n\n        let mut headers = HashMap::new();\n        headers.insert(\"X-Tenant\".to_string(), \"tenant-123\".to_string());\n\n        let tenant = manager.resolve_tenant(&headers, None);\n        assert_eq!(tenant, Some(\"tenant-123\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_resolve_tenant_missing_header() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 3, 1));\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(TenantAffinityConfig::default(), topology);\n\n        let headers = HashMap::new();\n        let tenant = manager.resolve_tenant(&headers, None);\n        assert!(tenant.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_static_map_override() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 3, 1));\n        let mut static_map = HashMap::new();\n        static_map.insert(\"enterprise-co\".to_string(), 0);\n\n        let config = TenantAffinityConfig {\n            static_map,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(config, topology);\n\n        let group = manager.get_group_for_tenant(\"enterprise-co\");\n        assert_eq!(group, Some(0));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_hash_based_assignment() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 5, 1));\n        let config = TenantAffinityConfig::default();\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(config, topology);\n\n        // Same tenant should always resolve to same group\n        let group1 = manager.get_group_for_tenant(\"tenant-abc\");\n        let group2 = manager.get_group_for_tenant(\"tenant-abc\");\n        assert_eq!(group1, group2);\n\n        // Different tenants should (usually) resolve to different groups\n        let group3 = manager.get_group_for_tenant(\"tenant-xyz\");\n        // Not guaranteed to be different due to hash collision possibility,\n        // but with 5 groups and good hash distribution, it's likely\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_is_dedicated_group() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 3, 1));\n        let config = TenantAffinityConfig {\n            dedicated_groups: vec![0],\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(config, topology);\n\n        assert!(manager.is_dedicated_group(0));\n        assert!(!manager.is_dedicated_group(1));\n        assert!(!manager.is_dedicated_group(2));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_tenant_allowed_in_dedicated_group() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 3, 1));\n        let mut static_map = HashMap::new();\n        static_map.insert(\"big-corp\".to_string(), 0);\n\n        let config = TenantAffinityConfig {\n            dedicated_groups: vec![0],\n            static_map,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(config, topology);\n\n        // big-corp is allowed in group 0 (dedicated)\n        assert!(manager.is_tenant_allowed_in_group(\"big-corp\", 0));\n\n        // big-corp is not allowed in group 1 (not dedicated, but not their mapped group)\n        // Actually, non-dedicated groups allow anyone\n        assert!(manager.is_tenant_allowed_in_group(\"big-corp\", 1));\n\n        // Other tenants are not allowed in group 0 (dedicated)\n        assert!(!manager.is_tenant_allowed_in_group(\"other-tenant\", 0));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_fallback_reject() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 3, 1));\n        let config = TenantAffinityConfig {\n            fallback: \"reject\".to_string(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(config, topology);\n\n        let headers = HashMap::new();\n        let result = manager.resolve_group(&headers, None);\n        assert!(result.is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_fallback_hash() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 3, 1));\n        let config = TenantAffinityConfig {\n            fallback: \"hash\".to_string(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(config, topology);\n\n        let headers = HashMap::new();\n        let result = manager.resolve_group(&headers, None);\n        assert!(result.is_ok());\n        // Should resolve to some group 0-2\n        if let Ok(Some(group)) = result {\n            assert!(group < 3);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_api_key_mapping() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 3, 1));\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(TenantAffinityConfig::default(), topology);\n\n        manager.set_api_key_mapping(\"key-123\".to_string(), \"tenant-abc\".to_string()).await;\n\n        let tenant = manager.get_tenant_for_api_key(\"key-123\").await;\n        assert_eq!(tenant, Some(\"tenant-abc\".to_string()));\n\n        let tenant = manager.get_tenant_for_api_key(\"key-unknown\").await;\n        assert!(tenant.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_resolve_disabled() {\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 3, 1));\n        let config = TenantAffinityConfig {\n            enabled: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let manager = TenantAffinityManager::new(config, topology);\n\n        let headers = HashMap::new();\n        let result = manager.resolve_tenant(&headers, None);\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":471,"startLine":1,"totalLines":471}}}

'static,\n62\t        F::Output: Send + 'static,\n63\t    {\n64\t        // Spawn a dedicated thread to run the async future\n65\t        // This avoids conflicts with any existing tokio runtime\n66\t   }\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t/// Redis-backed TaskStore.\n79\t#[derive(Clone)]\n80\tpub struct RedisTaskStore {\n81\t    /// Redis connection pool\n82\t    pool: RedisPool,\n83\t    /// Key prefix for all Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes).ok().map(String::from),\n173\t            Value::Int(i) => Some(i.to_string()),\n174\t            Value::SimpleString(s) => &[\n279\t                    (\"miroir_id\", task.miroir_id.as_str()),\n280\t                    (\"created_at\", created_at_str.as_str()),\n281\t                    (\"status\", Redis-backed TaskStore implementation (plan §4 \"Redis mode (HA)\").\n//!\n//! This module implements the TaskStore trait using Redis as the backend.\n//! Each SQLite table is mapped to a Redis keyspace as specified in plan §4.\n\nuse crate::task_store::*;\nuse crate::Result;\nuse crate::MiroirError;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::SystemTime;\nuse tokio::sync::Mutex;\n\nuse ::redis::aio::ConnectionManager;\nuse ::redis::{\n    pipe, AsyncCommands, Client, ExistenceCheck, FromRedisValue, Pipeline, SetExpiry,\n    SetOptions, Value,\n};\nuse futures_util::StreamExt;\n\n\n/// Redis connection pool wrapper.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct RedisPool {\n    /// Connection manager for async operations (shared across clones)\n    manager: Arc<Mutex<ConnectionManager>>,\n}\n\nimpl RedisPool {\n    /// Create a new Redis pool from a connection URL.\n    pub async fn new(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let client = Client::open(url).map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n        let conn = client\n            .get_connection_manager()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        Ok(Self {\n            manager: Arc::new(Mutex::new(conn)),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a pipeline and return its query result.\n    pub async fn pipeline_query<R>(&self, pipe: &mut Pipeline) -> Result<R>\n    where\n        R: FromRedisValue,\n    {\n        let mut conn = self.manager.lock().await;\n        pipe.query_async(&mut *conn)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n\n    /// Block on an async future using a dedicated runtime.\n    /// Spawns a dedicated thread with its own single-threaded runtime to avoid\n    /// \"cannot start a runtime from within a runtime\" panics when called from\n    /// within an existing tokio runtime (e.g., in tests).\n    fn block_on<F>(&self, future: F) -> F::Output\n    where\n        F: std::future::Future + Send + 'static,\n        F::Output: Send + 'static,\n    {\n        // Spawn a dedicated thread to run the async future\n        // This avoids conflicts with any existing tokio runtime\n        std::thread::spawn(move || {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n                .enable_all()\n                .build()\n                .expect(\"Failed to create runtime in thread\");\n            rt.block_on(future)\n        })\n        .join()\n        .unwrap_or_else(|_| panic!(\"block_on thread panicked\"))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Redis-backed TaskStore.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct RedisTaskStore {\n    /// Redis connection pool\n    pool: RedisPool,\n    /// Key prefix for all Miroir keys\n    key_prefix: String,\n}\n\nimpl RedisTaskStore {\n    /// Open a Redis task store from a connection URL.\n    pub async fn open(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let pool = RedisPool::new(url).await?;\n        Ok(Self {\n            pool,\n            key_prefix: \"miroir\".into(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Return the key prefix used by this store.\n    pub fn key_prefix(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.key_prefix\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a fully-qualified Redis key.\n    fn key(&self, parts: &[&str]) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}:{}\", self.key_prefix, parts.join(\":\"))\n    }\n\n    /// Helper: run an async future using the dedicated runtime.\n    fn block_on<F>(&self, future: F) -> F::Output\n    where\n        F: std::future::Future + Send + 'static,\n        F::Output: Send + 'static,\n    {\n        self.pool.block_on(future)\n    }\n\n    /// Helper: parse a hash row into a TaskRow.\n    fn task_from_hash(\n        miroir_id: String,\n        fields: &HashMap<String, Value>,\n    ) -> Result<TaskRow> {\n        let created_at = get_field_i64(fields, \"created_at\")?;\n        let status = get_field_string(fields, \"status\")?;\n        let node_tasks_json = get_field_string(fields, \"node_tasks\")?;\n        let node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64> = serde_json::from_str(&node_tasks_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid node_tasks JSON: {e}\")))?;\n        let error = opt_field(fields, \"error\");\n        let started_at = opt_field_i64(fields, \"started_at\");\n        let finished_at = opt_field_i64(fields, \"finished_at\");\n        let index_uid = opt_field(fields, \"index_uid\");\n        let task_type = opt_field(fields, \"task_type\");\n        let node_errors_json = opt_field(fields, \"node_errors\").unwrap_or_else(|| \"{}\".to_string());\n        let node_errors: HashMap<String, String> = serde_json::from_str(&node_errors_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid node_errors JSON: {e}\")))?;\n\n        Ok(TaskRow {\n            miroir_id,\n            created_at,\n            status,\n            node_tasks,\n            error,\n            started_at,\n            finished_at,\n            index_uid,\n            task_type,\n            node_errors,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Helper: parse canary hash row.\n    fn canary_from_hash(\n        id: String,\n        fields: &HashMap<String, Value>,\n    ) -> Result<CanaryRow> {\n        Ok(CanaryRow {\n            id,\n            name: get_field_string(fields, \"name\")?,\n            index_uid: get_field_string(fields, \"index_uid\")?,\n            interval_s: get_field_i64(fields, \"interval_s\")?,\n            query_json: get_field_string(fields, \"query_json\")?,\n            assertions_json: get_field_string(fields, \"assertions_json\")?,\n            enabled: get_field_i64(fields, \"enabled\")? != 0,\n            created_at: get_field_i64(fields, \"created_at\")?,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Helper: get a string field from a Redis hash.\nfn get_field_string(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Result<String> {\n    fields\n        .get(key)\n        .and_then(|v| match v {\n            Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes).ok().map(String::from),\n            Value::Int(i) => Some(i.to_string()),\n            Value::SimpleString(s) => Some(s.clone()),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n        .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"missing field: {key}\")))\n}\n\n/// Helper: get an i64 field from a Redis hash.\nfn get_field_i64(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Result<i64> {\n    fields\n        .get(key)\n        .and_then(|v| match v {\n            Value::Int(i) => Some(*i),\n            Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes)\n                .ok()\n                .and_then(|s| s.parse::<i64>().ok()),\n            Value::SimpleString(s) => s.parse::<i64>().ok(),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n        .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"missing or invalid field: {key}\")))\n}\n\n/// Helper: convert optional field to Option<String>.\nfn opt_field(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    fields.get(key).and_then(|v| match v {\n        Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes).ok().map(String::from),\n        Value::Int(i) => Some(i.to_string()),\n        Value::SimpleString(s) => Some(s.clone()),\n        _ => None,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Helper: convert optional field to Option<i64>.\nfn opt_field_i64(fields: &HashMap<String, Value>, key: &str) -> Option<i64> {\n    fields.get(key).and_then(|v| match v {\n        Value::Int(i) => Some(*i),\n        Value::BulkString(bytes) => std::str::from_utf8(bytes)\n            .ok()\n            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<i64>().ok()),\n        Value::SimpleString(s) => s.parse::<i64>().ok(),\n        _ => None,\n    })\n}\n\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// TaskStore trait implementation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nimpl TaskStore for RedisTaskStore {\n    fn migrate(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let version_key = format!(\"{}:schema_version\", key_prefix);\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let current: Option<i64> = conn.get(&version_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let binary_version = crate::schema_migrations::build_registry()\n                .max_version();\n\n            // Validate that store version is not ahead of binary\n            if let Some(v) = current {\n                if v > binary_version {\n                    return Err(MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n                        store_version: v,\n                        binary_version,\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n\n            // If this is a fresh store, record our version\n            if current.is_none() {\n                let _: () = conn.set(&version_key, binary_version).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 1: tasks ---\n\n    fn insert_task(&self, task: &NewTask) -> Result<()> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let task = task.clone();\n self.block_on(async move {\n            let node_tasks_json = serde_json::to_string(&task.node_tasks)?;\n            let node_errors_json = serde_json::to_string(&task.node_errors)?;\n\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            pipe.hset_multiple(\n                &key,\n                &[\n                    (\"miroir_id\", task.miroir_id.as_str()),\n                    (\"created_at\", created_at_str.as_str()),\n                    (\"status\", task.status.as_str()),\n                    (\"node_tasks\", node_tasks_json.as_str()),\n                    (\"node_errors\", node_errors_json.as_str()),\n                ],\n            );\n            if let Some(ref error) = task.error {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"error\", error);\n            }\n            if let Some(started_at) = task.started_at {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"started_at\", started_at);\n            }\n            if let Some(finished_at) = task.finished_at {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"finished_at\", finished_at);\n            }\n            if let Some(ref index_uid) = task.index_uid {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"index_uid\", index_uid);\n            }\n            if let Some(ref task_type) = task.task_type {\n                pipe.hset(&key, \"task_type\", task_type);\n            }\n            pipe.sadd(&index_key, &task.miroir_id);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_task(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<TaskRow>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", miroir_id]);\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = {\n                Ok(None)\n            } else {\n                Ok(Some(Self::task_from_hash(miroir_id, &fields)?))\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_task_status(&self, miroir_id: self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let exists: bool = conn.hexists(&key, \"miroir_id\").await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if !exists {\n                return Ok(false);\n            }\n\n            let _: () = conn.hset(&key, \"status\", &status).await\n let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", miroir_id]);\n        let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut &str, error: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", miroir_id]);\n        let error = error.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let exists: bool = conn.hexists(&key, \"miroir_id\").await\n                .map_err(|e| .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            Ok(true)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn list_tasks(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<TaskRow>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let index_key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", \"_index\"]);\n        let status_filter = filter.status.clone();\n        let index_uid_filter = filter.index_uid.clone();\n        let task_type_filter = filter.task_type.clone();\n        let limit = filter.limit;\n        let offset = filter.offset;\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let all_ids: Vec<String> = conn.smembers(&index_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n            for miroir_id in all_ids {\n                let key = format!(\"{}:tasks:{}\", key_prefix, miroir_id);\n                let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n                if fields.is_empty() {\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                let task = Self::task_from_hash(miroir_id, &fields)?;\n\n                // Apply filters\n                if let Some(ref status) = status_filter {\n                    if &task.status != status {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n                if let Some(ref index_uid) = index_uid_filter {\n                    if task.index_uid.as_ref() != Some(index_uid) {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n                if let Some(ref task_type) = task_type_filter {\n                    if task.task_type.as_ref() != Some(task_type) {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                tasks.push(task);\n            }\n\n            // Sort by created_at DESC\n            tasks.sort_by(|a, b| b.created_at.cmp(&a.created_at));\n\n            // Apply pagination\n            if let Some(offset) = offset {\n                if offset < tasks.len() {\n                    tasks.drain(0..offset);\n                } else {\n                    tasks.clear();\n                }\n            }\n            if let Some(limit) = limit {\n                tasks.truncate(limit);\n            }\n\n            Ok(tasks)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn prune_tasks(&self, cutoff_ms: i64, batch_size: u32) -> Result<usize> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let index_key = self.key(&[\"tasks\", \"_index\"]);\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let all_ids: Vec<String> = conn.smembers(&index_key).await\n      for miroir_id in all_ids.into_iter().take(batch_size as usize) {\n                let key = format!(\"{}:tasks:{}\", key_prefix, miroir_id);\n\n                // Use a pipeline to get both fields atomically\n                let mut p = pipe();\n                p.hget(&key, \"created_at\");\n                p.hget(&key, \"status\");\n                let result: (Option<String>, Option<String>) = pool.pipeline_query(&mut p).await?;\n\n                if let (Some(created_at_str), Some(status)) = result {\n                    let created_at: i64 = created_at_str.parse()\n                        .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid created_at: {e}\")))?;\n                    if created_at < cutoff_ms && terminal_statuses.contains(&status.as_str()) {\n                        to_delete.push(miroir_id);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            if to_delete.is_empty() {\n                return Ok(0);\n            }\n\n     .map(|uids| serde_json::to_string(uids))\n            .transpose()?\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n        let history_json = serde_json::to_string(&alias.history)?;\n        let version_str      } else {\n                let history_json = get_field_string(&fields, \"history\")?;\n                let history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)\n usize) -> Result<bool> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let name = name.to_string();\n        let new_uid = new_uid.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:aliases:{}\", key_prefix, name);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if fields.is_empty() {\n                return Ok(false);\n            }\n\n            let old_uid = opt_field(&fields, \"current_uid\").unwrap_or_default();\n            let old_version = get_field_i64(&fields, \"version\")?;\n            let history_json = get_field_string(&fields, \"history\")?;\n            let mut history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"invalid history JSON: {e}\")))?;\n\n            if !old_uid.is_empty() {\n                history.push(AliasHistoryEntry {\n                    uid: old_uid,\n                    flipped_at: now_ms(),\n                });\n            }\n            while history.len() > history_retention {\n                history.remove(0);\n            }\n\n            let new_history_json = serde_json::to_string(&history)?;\n            let new_version_str = (old_version + 1).to_string();\n\n            // Use pipeline_query for the atomic update\n &new_history_json);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n\n            Ok(true)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn delete_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let name = name.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:aliases:{}\", key_prefix, name);\n        let index_key =    }\n\n    fn list_jobs_by_state(&self, state: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let state = state.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut result = Vec::new();\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Use the _index set for O(cardinality) iteration (no SCAN).\n            let index_key = format!(\"{}:jobs:_index\", key_prefix);\n            let ids: Vec<String> = conn.smembers(&index_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            for id in ids {\n                let key = format!(\"{}:jobs:{}\", key_prefix, id);\n                let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n                if !fields.is_empty() {\n                    if let Ok(job_state) =    })\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 7: leader_lease ---\n\n    fn try_acquire_leader_lease(\n        &self,\n        scope: &str,\n        holder: &str,\n        expires_at: i64,\n        now_ms: i64,\n    ) -> Result<bool> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let scope = scope.to_string();\n        let holder = holder.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:lease:{}\", key_prefix, scope);\n        let ttl_seconds = ((expires_at - now_ms) / 1000).max(1) as u64;\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n\n            // SET NX EX — only set if not exists\n            let acquired: bool = {\n                let opts = SetOptions::default()\n                    .conditional_set(ExistenceCheck::NX)\n                    .with_expiration(SetExpiry::EX(ttl_seconds));\n                conn.set_options(&key, &holder, opts).await\n            }.map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if acquired {\n                return Ok(true);\n            }\n\n            // Check if we can steal the lease (expired or we hold it)\n            let current_holder: Option<String> = conn.get(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            match current_holder {\n                Some(h) if h == holder => {\n                    // We hold it — renew\n                    let opts = SetOptions::default()\n                        .conditional_set(ExistenceCheck::XX)\n                        .with_expiration(SetExpiry::EX(ttl_seconds));\n                    let _: () = conn.set_options(&key, &holder, opts).await\n                        .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n                    Ok(true)\n                }\n                Some(_) => {\n                    // Someone else holds it — check expiry using TTL\n                    let ttl: i64 = conn.ttl(&key).await\n                        .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n                    // TTL of -2 means key doesn't exist, -1 means no expiry\n                    if ttl == -2 || (ttl >= 0 && ttl <= (expires_at - now_ms) / 1000) {\n                        // Lease has expired — try to steal it\n                        let opts = SetOptions::default()\n                            .conditional_set(ExistenceCheck::NX)\n                            .with_expiration(SetExpiry::EX(ttl_seconds));\n                        let acquired: bool = conn.set_options(&key, &holder, opts).await\n                            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n                        Ok(acquired)\n                    } else {\n                        Ok(false)\n                    }\n                }\n                None => {\n                    // Key doesn't exist — acquire\n                    let opts = SetOptions::default()\n                        .conditional_set(ExistenceCheck::NX)\n                        .with_expiration(SetExpiry::EX(ttl_seconds));\n                    let acquired: bool = conn.set_options(&key, &holder, opts).await\n                        .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n                    Ok(acquired)\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn renew_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str, holder: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let scope = scope.to_string();\n        let holder = holder.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:lease:{}\", key_prefix, scope);\n        let ttl_seconds = ((expires_at - now_ms()) / 1000).max(1) as u64;\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n\n            // SET XX EX — only set if exists (we hold it)\n            let opts = SetOptions::default()\n                .conditional_set(ExistenceCheck::XX)\n                .with_expiration(SetExpiry::EX(ttl_seconds));\n            let renewed: bool = conn.set_options(&key, &holder, opts).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            Ok(renewed)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<Option<LeaderLeaseRow>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let scope = scope.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:lease:{}\", key_prefix, scope);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let holder: Option<String> = conn.get(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let Some(holder) = holder else {\n                return Ok(None);\n            };\n\n            // Get TTL to compute expires_at\n            let ttl: i64 = conn.ttl(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let expires_at = if ttl == -1 {\n                // No expiry set\n                i64::MAX\n            } else if ttl >= 0 {\n                now_ms() + ttl * 1000\n            } else {\n                // Key doesn't exist or expired\n                return Ok(None);\n            };\n\n            Ok(Some(LeaderLeaseRow {\n                scope: scope.clone(),\n                holder,\n                expires_at,\n            }))\n        })\n    }\n\n    // --- Tables 8-14: Feature-flagged tables ---\n\n    // --- Table 8: canaries ---\n\n    fn upsert_canary(&self, canary: &NewCanary) -> Result<()> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let canary = canary.clone();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:canary:{}\", key_prefix, canary.id);\n        let index_key = format!(\"{}:canary:_index\", key_prefix);\n\n        let interval_s_str = canary.interval_s.to_string();\n        let enabled_str = (canary.enabled as i64).to_string();\n        let created_at_str = canary.created_at.to_string();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            pipe.hset_multiple(\n                &key,\n                &[\n                    (\"id\", canary.id.as_str()),\n                    (\"name\", canary.name.as_str()),\n                    (\"index_uid\", canary.index_uid.as_str()),\n                    (\"interval_s\", interval_s_str.as_str()),\n                    (\"query_json\", canary.query_json.as_str()),\n                    (\"assertions_json\", canary.assertions_json.as_str()),\n                    (\"enabled\", enabled_str.as_str()),\n                    (\"created_at\", created_at_str.as_str()),\n                ],\n            );\n            pipe.sadd(&index_key, &canary.id);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_canary(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<CanaryRow>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let id = id.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:canary:{}\", key_prefix, id);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if fields.is_empty() {\n                Ok(None)\n            } else {\n                Ok(Some(Self::canary_from_hash(id.clone(), &fields)?))\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn list_canaries(&self) -> Result<Vec<CanaryRow>> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let index_key = format!(\"{}:canary:_index\", key_prefix);\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n            let ids: Vec<String> = conn.smembers(&index_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let mut result = Vec::new();\n            for id in ids {\n                let key = format!(\"{}:canary:{}\", key_prefix, id);\n                let fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&key).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n                if !fields.is_empty() {\n                    result.push(Self::canary_from_hash(id, &fields)?);\n                }\n            }\n\n            Ok(result)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn delete_canary(&self, id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let id = id.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:canary:{}\", key_prefix, id);\n        let index_key = format!(\"{}:canary:_index\", key_prefix);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n\n            let exists: bool = conn.exists(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if !exists {\n                return Ok(false);\n            }\n\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            pipe.del(&key);\n            pipe.srem(&index_key, &id);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut run.canary_id);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Add new run to sorted set (score = ran_at)\n            let value = serde_json::to_string(&run)?;\n            let _: () = conn.zadd(&key, run.ran_at, value).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            // Trim to keep only the most recent N runs using ZREMRANGEBYRANK\n            let start = 0isize;\n            let end = -(run_history_limit as isize) - 1;\n            let _: () = conn.zremrangebyrank(&key, start, format!(\"{}:canary_runs:{}\", key_prefix, canary_id);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Get runs in descending order by ran_at (most recent first)\n            let values: Vec<String> = conn.zrevrange(&key, 0, (limit as isize) - 1).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let mut }\n1752\t\n1753\t    fn revoke_admin_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n1754\t        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n1755\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n1756\t        let session_id = session_id.to_string();\n1757\t        let key = format!(\"{}:admin_session:{}\", key_prefix, session_id);\n1758\t        let channel = format!(\"{}:admin_session:revoked\", key_prefix);\n1759\t\n1760\t        self.block_on(async move {\n1761\t            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n1762\t\n1763\t            let exists: bool = conn.hexists(&key, \"session_id\").await\n1764\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1765\t\n1766\t            if !exists {\n1767\t                return Ok(false);\n1768\t            }\n1769\t\n1770\t            let _: () = conn.hset(&key, \"revoked\", 1i64).await\n1771\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1772\t\n1773\t            // Publish to revoked channel for immediate invalidation across pods\n1774\t            let _: () = conn.publish(&channel, &session_id).await\n1775\t                .map_err(|e|   }\n1786\t}\n1787\t\n1788\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1789\t// Extra Redis-specific keys (plan §4 footnotes)\n1790\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1791\t\n1792\timpl RedisTaskStore {\n1793\t    // --- Rate limiting: search_ui ---\n1794\t\n1795\t    /// Check and increment rate limit counter for search UI access.\n1796\t    /// Returns (allowed, remaining_requests, reset_after_seconds).\n1797\t    pub fn check_rate_limit_searchui(\n1798\t        &self,\n1799\t        ip: &str,\n1800\t        limit: u64,\n1801\t        window_seconds: u64,\n1802\t    ) -> Result<(bool, u64, i64)> {\n1803\t        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n1804\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n1805\t        let ip = ip.to_string();\n1806\t        let key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:searchui:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n1807\t\n1808\t        self.block_on(async move {\n1809\t            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n1810\t\n1811\t            // Get current count\n1812\t            let current: Option<u64> = conn.get(&key).await\n1813\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1814\t\n1815\t            // Get TTL\n1816\t            let ttl: i64 = conn.ttl(&key).await\n1817\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1818\t\n1819\t            let count = current.unwrap_or(0);\n1820\t\n1821\t            // Check if limit exceeded\n1822\t            if count >= limit {\n1823\t                return Ok((false, 0, ttl.max(0)));\n1824\t            }\n1825\t\n1826\t            // Increment counter\n1827\t            let new_count: u64 = conn.incr(&key, 1).await\n1828\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1829\t\n1830\t            // Set expiry on first request\n1831\t            if count == 0 {\n1832\t                let _: () = conn.expire(&key, window_seconds as i64).await\n1833\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1834\t            }\n1835\t\n1836\t            Ok((true, limit.saturating_sub(new_count), ttl.max(0)))\n1837\t        })\n1838\t    }\n1839\t\n1840\t    // --- Rate limiting: admin_login ---\n1841\t\n1842\t    /// Check admin login rate limit and exponential backoff.\n1843\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n1844\t    pub fn check_rate_limit_admin_login(\n1845\t        &self,\n1846\t        ip: &str,\n1847\t        limit: u64,\n1848\t        window_seconds: u64,\n1849\t    ) -> Result<(bool, Option<u64>)> {\n1850\t        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n1851\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n1852\t        let ip = ip.to_string();\n1853\t        let backoff_key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n1854\t        let key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n1855\t\n1856\t        self.block_on(async move {\n1857\t            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n1858\t\n1859\t            // Check if we're in backoff mode\n1860\t            let backoff_fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&backoff_key).await\n1861\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1862\t\n1863\t            if !backoff_fields.is_empty() {\n1864\t                let next_allowed_at = get_field_i64(&backoff_fields, \"next_allowed_at\")?;\n1865\t                let now = now_ms();\n1866\t                if next_allowed_at > now {\n1867\t                    let wait_seconds = ((next_allowed_at - now) / 1000) as u64;\n1868\t                    return Ok((false, Some(wait_seconds)));\n1869\t                }\n1870\t                // Backoff expired, clear it\n1871\t                let _: () = conn.del(&backoff_key).await\n1872\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1873\t            }\n1874\t\n1875\t            // Check standard rate limit\n1876\t            let current: Option<u64> = conn.get(&key).await\n1877\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1878\t\n1879\t            let count = current.unwrap_or(0);\n1880\t\n1881\t            // Check if limit exceeded\n1882\t            if count >= limit {\n1883\t                return Ok((false, None));\n1884\t            }\n1885\t\n1886\t            // Increment counter\n1887\t            let mut pipe = pipe();\n1888\t            pipe.incr(&key, 1);\n1889\t            pipe.expire(&key, window_seconds as i64);\n1890\t            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n1891\t\n1892\t            Ok((true, None))\n1893\t        })\n1894\t    }\n1895\t\n1896\t    /// Record a failed admin login attempt and return backoff if triggered.\n1897\t    /// Returns Some(wait_seconds) if backoff was triggered, None otherwise.\n1898\t    pub fn record_failure_admin_login(\n1899\t        &self,\n1900\t        ip: &str,\n1901\t        failed_threshold: u32,\n1902\t        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n1903\t        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n1904\t    ) -> Result<Option<u64>> {\n1905\t        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n1906\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n1907\t        let ip = ip.to_string();\n1908\t        let backoff_key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n1909\t\n1910\t        self.block_on(async move {\n1911\t            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n1912\t\n1913\t            // Check if already in backoff\n1914\t            let backoff_fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&backoff_key).await\n1915\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1916\t\n1917\t            let current_failed: u64 = if backoff_fields.is_empty() {\n1918\t                0\n1919\t            } else {\n1920\t                get_field_i64(&backoff_fields, \"failed_count\")? as u64\n1921\t            };\n1922\t\n1923\t            let new_failed = current_failed + 1;\n1924\t\n1925\t            // Check if we should enter backoff mode\n1926\t            if new_failed >= failed_threshold as u64 {\n1927\t                let backoff_exponent = (new_failed.saturating_sub(failed_threshold as u64) as u32).min(7);\n1928\t                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << backoff_exponent);\n1929\t                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n1930\t\n1931\t                let now = now_ms();\n1932\t                let next_allowed_at = now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000);\n1933\t\n1934\t                let mut pipe = pipe();\n1935\t                pipe.hset(&backoff_key, \"failed_count\", new_failed as i64);\n1936\t                pipe.hset(&backoff_key, \"next_allowed_at\", next_allowed_at);\n1937\t                pipe.expire(&backoff_key, (backoff_seconds as i64 + 60) as i64);\n1938\t                pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n1939\t\n1940\t                return Ok(Some(backoff_seconds));\n1941\t            }\n1942\t\n1943\t            // Just update the failed count\n1944\t            let _: () = conn.hset(&backoff_key, \"failed_count\", new_failed as i64).await\n1945\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n1946\t\n1947\t            Ok(None)\n1948\t        })\n1949\t    }\n1950\t\n1951\t    /// Reset admin login rate limit on successful login.\n1952\t    pub fn reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&self, ip: &str) -> Result<()> {\n1953\t        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n1954\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n1955\t        let ip = ip.to_string();\n1956\t        let key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:{}\", })\n1966\t    }\n1967\t\n1968\t    // --- search_ui rate limit ---\n1969\t\n1970\t    /// Check search UI rate limit for a given IP.\n1971\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n1972\t    /// Uses a simple INCR + EXPIRE pattern for sliding window.\n1973\t    pub fn check_rate_limit_search_ui(\n1974\t        &self,\n1975\t        ip: &str,\n1976\t        limit: u64,\n1977\t        window_seconds: u64,\n1978\t    ) -> Result<(bool, Option<u64>)> {\n1979\t        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n1980\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n1981\t        let ip = ip.to_string();\n1982\t        let key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:searchui:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n1983\t\n1984\t        self.block_on(async move {\n1985\t            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n1986\t\n1987\t            // Check current count\n1988\t            let current: Option<u64> = conn.get(&key).await\n1989\t                .map_err(|e| fn insert_admin_session(&self, session: &NewAdminSession) -> Result<()> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let session = \"admin_key_hash\", &session.admin_key_hash);\n            pipe.hset(&key, \"created_at\", &created_at_str);\n            pipe.hset(&key, \"expires_at\", &expires_at_str);\n            pipe.hset(&key, \"revoked\", revoked_str);\n            pipe.expire(&key, ttl_seconds as i64);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n\n            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n session_id = session_id.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:admin_session:{}\", key_prefix, session_id);\n        let channel = format!(\"{}:admin_session:revoked\", key_prefix);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n\n            let exists: bool = conn.hexists(&key, \"session_id\").await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if !exists {\n                return Ok(false);\n            }\n\n            let _: () = conn.hset(&key, \"revoked\", 1i64).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            // Publish to revoked channel for immediate invalidation across pods\n            let _: () = conn.publish(&channel, &session_id).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            Ok(true)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn delete_expired_admin_sessions(&self, _now_ms: i64) -> ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nimpl RedisTaskStore {\n    // --- Rate limiting: search_ui ---\n\n    /// Check and increment rate limit counter for search UI access.\n    /// Returns (allowed, remaining_requests, reset_after_seconds).\n    pub fn check_rate_limit_searchui(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        limit: u64,\n        window_seconds: u64,\n    ) -> Result<(bool, u64, i64)> {\n        let manager = self.pool.manager.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let ip = ip.to_string();\n        let key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:searchui:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Get current count\n            let current: Option<u64> = conn.get(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            // Get TTL\n            let ttl: i64 = conn.ttl(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let count = current.unwrap_or(0);\n\n            // Check if limit exceeded\n            if count >= limit {\n                return Ok((false, 0, ttl.max(0)));\n            }\n\n            // Increment counter\n            let new_count: u64 = conn.incr(&key, 1).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            // Set expiry on first request\n            if count == 0 {\n                let _: () = conn.expire(&key, window_seconds as i64).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            }\n\n            Ok((true, limit.saturating_sub(new_count), ttl.max(0)))\n        })\n    }\n\n    // --- Rate limiting: admin_login ---\n\n    /// Check admin login rate limit and exponential backoff.\n    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n    pub fn check_rate_limit_admin_login(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        limit: u64,\n        window_seconds: u64,\n    ) -> Result<(bool, Option<u64>)> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let ip = ip.to_string();\n        let backoff_key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n        let key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Check if we're in backoff mode\n            let backoff_fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&backoff_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if !backoff_fields.is_empty() {\n                let next_allowed_at = get_field_i64(&backoff_fields, \"next_allowed_at\")?;\n                let now = now_ms();\n                if next_allowed_at > now {\n                    let wait_seconds = ((next_allowed_at - now) / 1000) as u64;\n                    return Ok((false, Some(wait_seconds)));\n                }\n                // Backoff expired, clear it\n                let _: () = conn.del(&backoff_key).await\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n            }\n\n            // Check standard rate limit\n            let current: Option<u64> = conn.get(&key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let count = current.unwrap_or(0);\n\n            // Check if limit exceeded\n            if count >= limit {\n                return Ok((false, None));\n            }\n\n            // Increment counter\n            let mut pipe = pipe();\n            pipe.incr(&key, 1);\n            pipe.expire(&key, window_seconds as i64);\n            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n\n            Ok((true, None))\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed admin login attempt and return backoff if triggered.\n    /// Returns Some(wait_seconds) if backoff was triggered, None otherwise.\n    pub fn record_failure_admin_login(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        failed_threshold: u32,\n        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n    ) -> Result<Option<u64>> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let ip = ip.to_string();\n        let backoff_key = format!(\"{}:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:{}\", key_prefix, ip);\n\n        self.block_on(async move {\n            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n\n            // Check if already in backoff\n            let backoff_fields: HashMap<String, Value> = conn.hgetall(&backoff_key).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            let current_failed: u64 = if backoff_fields.is_empty() {\n                0\n            } else {\n                get_field_i64(&backoff_fields, \"failed_count\")? as u64\n            };\n\n            let new_failed = current_failed + 1;\n\n            // Check if we should enter backoff mode\n            if new_failed >= failed_threshold as u64 {\n                let backoff_exponent = (new_failed.saturating_sub(failed_threshold as u64) as u32).min(7);\n                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << backoff_exponent);\n                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n\n                let now = now_ms();\n                let next_allowed_at = now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000);\n\n                let mut pipe = pipe();\n                pipe.hset(&backoff_key, \"failed_count\", new_failed as i64);\n                pipe.hset(&backoff_key, \"next_allowed_at\", next_allowed_at);\n                pipe.expire(&backoff_key, (backoff_seconds as i64 + 60) as i64);\n                pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n\n                return Ok(Some(backoff_seconds));\n            }\n\n            // Just update the failed count\n            let _: () = conn.hset(&backoff_key, \"failed_count\", new_failed as i64).await\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            Ok(None)\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Reset admin login rate limit on successful login.\n    pub fn reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&self, ip: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n        let ip = ip.to_string();\n overflow buffer ---\n2216\t\n2217\t    /// Append to the CDC overflow buffer for a sink.\n2218\t    /// Uses LPUSH + LTRIM to keep the list bounded by byte budget.\n2219\t    /// Returns (current_element_count, was_trimmed).\n2220\t    pub fn cdc_overflow_append(\n2221\t        &self,\n2222\t        sink_name: &str,\n2223\t        data: &[u8],\n2224\t        max_bytes: usize,\n2225\t    ) -> Result<(usize, bool)> {\n2226\t        let pool = self.pool.clone();\n2227\t        let key_prefix = self.key_prefix.clone();\n2228\t        let sink_name = sink_name.to_string();\n2229\t        let data = data.to_vec();\n2230\t        let key = format!(\"{}:cdc:overflow:{}\", key_prefix, sink_name);\n2231\t        let bytes_key = format!(\"{}:cdc:overflow_bytes:{}\", key_prefix, sink_name);\n2232\t        let data_len = data.len();\n2233\t\n2234\t        self.block_on(async move {\n2235\t            let mut conn = pool.manager.lock().await;\n2236\t\n2237\t            // Read tracked byte size (atomic counter in a separate key)\n2238\t            let tracked_bytes: i64 = conn.get(&bytes_key).await\n2239\t                .unwrap_or(None)\n2240\t                .unwrap_or(0);\n2241\t\n2242\t            let new_bytes = tracked_bytes + data_len as i64;\n2243\t            let mut trimmed = false;\n2244\t\n2245\t            // If adding this event exceeds the budget, trim from the tail (oldest)\n2246\t            // until we are back under budget.\n2247\t            if new_bytes > max_bytes as i64 {\n2248\t                let current_len: i64 = conn.llen(&key).await\n2249\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n2250\t\n2251\t                // Estimate elements to keep: proportional to remaining budget.\n2252\t                if current_len > 0 && tracked_bytes > 0 {\n2253\t                    let avg_element_bytes = tracked_bytes as f64 / current_len as f64;\n2254\t                    let keep = ((max_bytes as f64) / avg_element_bytes).floor() as isize;\n2255\t                    if keep > 0 {\n2256\t                        let _: () = conn.ltrim(&key, 0, keep - 1).await\n2257\t                            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n2258\t                    } else {\n2259\t                        let _: () = conn.del(&key).await\n2260\t                            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n2261\t                    }\n2262\t                }\n2263\t                trimmed = true;\n2264\t            }\n2265\t\n2266\t            // LPUSH new element to the head (newest first)\n2267\t            let _: () = conn.lpush(&key, &data).await\n2268\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n2269\t\n2270\t            // Update byte counter: recompute from LLEN * average or just add\n2271\t            // the new element's bytes (exact enough for overflow purposes).\n2272\t            let final_count: i64 = conn.llen(&key).await\n2273\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Redis(e.to_string()))?;\n2274\t\n2275\t            // If we trimmed, recompute tracked bytes from scratch; otherwise add.\n2276\t            let new_tracked = if trimmed {\n2277\t                // Approximate: element_count * new_element_bytes is a rough\n2278\t                // lower bound.  For a tighter number we'd need LRANGE + sum,\n2279\t                // but for overflow budgeting this is sufficient.\n2280\t                (final_count as f64 * data_len as f64) as i64\n2281\t            } else {\n2282\t                tracked_bytes + data_len as i64\n2283\t            };\n2284\t\n2285\t            let mut pipe = pipe();\n2286\t            pipe.set(&bytes_key, new_tracked);\n2287\t            pool.pipeline_query::<()>(&mut pipe).await?;\n2288\t\n2289\t            Ok((final_count as usize, trimmed))\n2290\t        })\n2291\t    }\n2292\t\n2293\t    /// Pop
